task: I’m trying to plan a Yorkshire family day out around Christmas and want to compare two festive options properly before I recommend one. Could you start on the official Stockeld Park site and open their Winter or Christmas ticket page, then the main activities page if needed, and pull together exactly what’s included with a standard festive ticket so I can see whether it feels like a full day out for kids; if there are photos or activity sections on the page, open the main ticket page and the activities/details page in separate tabs and leave them open so I can glance at them later. Then use Google to find the current Harrogate Christmas Funland page or official event listing, open the actual event page, and summarize what’s included there too, along with the location and the event dates, because I want to compare whether it sounds more substantial than Stockeld Park; if you find more than one relevant result, open the most official-looking listing in its own tab and verify it’s live before using it. After that, go to the official York Maze site and pull together the practical visitor details for using it as a backup daytime activity nearby, especially where it is, the opening times or seasonal opening info, and any important ticket or visit-planning notes like booking ahead, age guidance, or whether it’s seasonal, and leave the key visitor info page open as well. In the end, give me a concise side-by-side comparison of Stockeld Park versus Harrogate Christmas Funland, then a short recommendation on which festive option seems better for a family day out and whether York Maze sounds like a realistic backup plan.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent successfully navigated to the official Stockeld Park website, specifically the Christmas ticket options page (https://stockeldpark.co.uk/plan-your-day/tickets-prices/christmas-ticket-options/) and the Christmas activities page (https://stockeldpark.co.uk/activities/season/christmas/). It used `webfetch` to extract the specific inclusions for the different ticket tiers (Gold, Silver, etc.), which included items like Santa's Grotto, Winter Illuminations, the Snowflake Maze, and the Enchanted Forest. The agent then summarized these findings in its final response. The requirement to accurately summarize the activities using the official pages is satisfied.

R2: success - The agent successfully used Google to find the Harrogate Christmas Funland event page (specifically the Great Yorkshire Events Centre listing and the Crow Leisure Group ticket page). It verified the content of these pages, noting that the available information was for the 2025 season, and successfully extracted the included features (unlimited rides, ice rink, Santa's Grotto, mini golf, etc.) as required by the rubric. The pages were opened and remained accessible for the user.

R3: success - The agent successfully navigated to the official event listing for "Santa's Christmas Funland" at the Great Yorkshire Events Centre (https://greatyorkshireeventscentre.co.uk/yec-events/santas-christmas-funland-harrogate/). The page clearly displays the location ("Great Yorkshire Events Centre, Great Yorkshire Showground, Harrogate, HG2 8NZ") and the event dates (December 13th – December 24th, as confirmed in the webfetch results and the agent's final summary). The rubric requirement to include the location and dates is satisfied.

R4: success - The agent successfully navigated to the official York Maze website (`yorkmaze.com`) and accessed the "Plan a Visit" section, specifically the "Times & Prices" and "Frequently Asked Questions" pages. Through these pages, the agent retrieved the necessary visitor details: location (Elvington Lane, York, YO19 5LT), opening times (10am - 6:30pm, last admission 3pm), and important visit-planning notes (tickets must be booked online, it is an outdoor attraction, and it is seasonal, running from July to September). The agent confirmed these details in its final summary and left the relevant pages open for the user.

R5: success - The agent provided a clear side-by-side comparison table in its final response (Step 26). This table directly compares Stockeld Park and Harrogate Christmas Funland across key categories: format, dates, standard inclusions, and paid extras. The comparison is concise and focuses on the suitability for a family festive outing, as requested. The agent successfully synthesized the information gathered from the various tabs it opened during the process.

R6: success - The agent provided a side-by-side comparison of Stockeld Park and Harrogate Christmas Funland, including details on format, dates, and inclusions. It also provided a recommendation: Stockeld Park is recommended for a "full day out" due to its expansive outdoor nature and variety of activities, whereas Harrogate Christmas Funland is better for a shorter, weather-proof, high-energy session. It also addressed the York Maze as a backup, noting that it is a seasonal summer attraction (July–September) and therefore not a realistic backup for a Christmas-time visit. The recommendation is reasoned and grounded in the gathered information.
